mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in industrial production over 3 months indicates rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts and consumer spending signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in joint rising demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 4.5% signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the unemployment rate and rise in payroll employment signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% and PAYEMS rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in joint tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP below 2% annualized growth rate signals a slowing economy, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls below 2% annualized growth rate
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
